name: Indiana Academy for Science, Mathematics, and Humanities
about: The Indiana Academy for Science, Mathematics, and Humanities is a nationally ranked public residential magnet high school for gifted and talented students, located on the campus of Ball State University in Muncie, Indiana. Founded in 1988, it serves high-ability students from across the state, primarily juniors and seniors, with a small non-residential sophomore program. It is tuition-free, and students may take AP, dual-credit, and college-level courses at Ball State. For the Class of 2024 the average SAT score was 1265.
college_matriculation: Nearly 95% of graduates attend four-year colleges or post-secondary education
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public school; residential program funded by the state of Indiana
extracurriculars: IHSAA athletics via partnership with Burris Laboratory School
Dual-credit courses at Ball State University

name: La Lumiere School
about: La Lumiere School is an independent, coeducational, Catholic college-preparatory boarding and day school in La Porte County, Indiana, founded in 1963. Its roughly 190-acre campus sits among two lakes and woodlands, and it serves students in grades 9-12 from the local area and around the world. The school is known for a strong athletics program, particularly basketball, which has produced multiple NBA players.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS); North Central Association
notable_alumni: John Roberts (Chief Justice of the United States), Jim Gaffigan (comedian/actor), Jordan Poole (NBA), Jaren Jackson Jr. (NBA), Jeremy Sochan (NBA)

name: Maharishi School of the Age of Enlightenment
about: Maharishi School is an independent, non-denominational, college-preparatory school in Fairfield, Iowa, founded in 1974 and built around consciousness-based education that incorporates the twice-daily practice of Transcendental Meditation. It serves preschool through grade 12 as a day school, with an on-campus boarding option for students in grades 9-12. The school draws a diverse community from more than 30 countries and reports that about 95% of graduates attend college.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS)
notable_alumni: Ben Foster (actor), Claire Hoffman (journalist), Eli Lieb (singer-songwriter)

name: Scattergood Friends School
about: Scattergood Friends School is a small Quaker coeducational boarding and day school in rural Cedar County, Iowa, near West Branch, founded in 1890 by Conservative (Wilburite) Friends. The school is guided by Quaker values of simplicity, peace, integrity, community, equality, and stewardship, and enrolls roughly 45 students, the majority of whom board. About a quarter of students come from outside the United States, and the school is historically notable for operating the Scattergood Hostel (1939-1943), which sheltered European refugees fleeing Nazi persecution.
accreditation: Independent Schools Association of the Central States (ISACS)
notable_alumni: Gonzalo Sanchez de Lozada (former President of Bolivia)
financial_aid_notes: Financial aid extended to over half of students

name: Maur Hill Mount Academy
about: Maur Hill-Mount Academy is a coeducational Catholic, Benedictine college-preparatory boarding and day high school in Atchison, Kansas, on a roughly 90-acre campus. It was formed in 2003 by the merger of Maur Hill Prep School (founded 1919, for boys) and Mount St. Scholastica Academy (founded 1863, for girls), and is sponsored by St. Benedict's Abbey and Mount St. Scholastica. The school enrolls students from across the United States and abroad and offers regular, honors, AP, and college-level coursework, including classes at nearby Benedictine College.
notable_alumni: Mike Haverty (former Kansas City Southern Railway CEO), Deran Sarafian (television producer/director)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based financial aid available; international boarding tuition approximately $44,350

name: St. John's Military School
about: St. John's Military School was a private, all-boys Episcopal military boarding school in Salina, Kansas, founded in 1887 by Elisha Smith Thomas, Bishop of Kansas. It served cadets in grades 6-12 and was known for one of the highest-ranked Army JROTC programs in the United States. The school closed after the 2018-2019 academic year amid declining enrollment and allegations of abuse; the campus was later acquired by Saint Francis Ministries.
accreditation: AdvancED; Kansas State Department of Education
notable_alumni: Nicholas Dockery (Medal of Honor recipient), Dean Hargrove (television producer/writer)
financial_aid_notes: School closed after the 2018-2019 academic year

name: Thomas More Prep-Marian
affiliation: Roman Catholic (Capuchin Franciscan / Diocese of Salina)
about: Thomas More Prep-Marian is a coeducational Catholic college-preparatory junior/senior high school in Hays, Kansas, serving grades 6-12 with both day and boarding options. Its roots trace to Hays Catholic College, founded in 1908 by the Capuchin Franciscans; the present school was formed through consolidation of earlier institutions and operates under the Roman Catholic Diocese of Salina. The school's teams compete as the Monarchs.
notable_alumni: John L. Allen Jr. (journalist), Jeff Colyer (Governor of Kansas), David J. Tholen (astronomer)
financial_aid_notes: No tuition for stewardship families in a Heartland Parish (fees still apply); aid available to others

name: Oneida Baptist Institute
about: Oneida Baptist Institute is a coeducational Southern Baptist boarding school in Oneida, Clay County, Kentucky, affiliated with the Kentucky Baptist Convention. Founded in 1899 by James Anderson Burns to bring education and reconciliation to a feud-torn region, it accepts day students in grades K-12 and boarding students in grades 9-12. Roughly half of students come from Kentucky, about 30% from other states, and 20% from other nations; fees are income-based and heavily subsidized, and no American student is turned away for inability to pay.
notable_alumni: Bert Combs (50th Governor of Kentucky), Jensen Huang (co-founder of Nvidia), Russell Coleman (Kentucky Attorney General)
financial_aid_notes: Income-based tuition; virtually every U.S. student receives a reduction; no American student turned away for inability to pay
